observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
a. City Accountant to:
 refrain from recording the procurement or receipt of supplies and materials to
expense account, but instead coursed them thru the inventory account in
consonance with the Perpetual Inventory System of accounting; and
 draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to recognize the expense based on the
Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) from the City General Services
Office.
b. City Accountant and City General Services Officer (CGSO) to:
 maintain a complete and updated Supplies Ledger Cards (SLCs) and Stock Cards
(SCs), respectively, for each inventory items;
 strictly conform to the provisions under Section 124 of the Manual on the New
Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs),
Volume I;
 conduct periodic reconciliation of records maintained by respective offices to
ensure that the balances reported are correct and accurate; and
 investigate the existence and condition of the non-moving merchandise
inventories valued at P4,700,658.37 and prepare the necessary report thereon;
c. CGSO to:
 prepare Requisition Issue Slip (RIS) for issuances of all supplies/materials and to
consolidate the issuances on a weekly basis using the SSMI and furnish the City
Accounting Office with copies thereof for recording in the books of cost of issued
items; and
 conduct a complete physical count of all inventories on semestral basis and
prepare and submit to the Office of the Auditor the Report of the Physical Count
of Inventories (RPCI) thereon, pursuant to Section 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs,
Volume I.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
 City Accountant and City General Services Officer to:
a. analyze and reconcile the material discrepancy of P447,614.236.48 between the
balance of the Subsidiary Ledger (SL) maintained by the City Accounting Office
(CAO) and the reported balance of the submitted Report on the Physical Count of
Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) by the City General Services Office
(CGSO), pursuant to Section C.3, Chapter V of the Manual on Property
Custodianship;
b. maintain complete and up-to-date Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards
(PPELCs) and Property Cards (PCs), in compliance with Section 114 of the
Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local
Government Units (LGUs), Volume I to facilitate reconciliation of record and
report; and
c. apply the provisions under COA Circular No. 2020-006 on the conduct of physical
count of PPE, recognition of PPE items found at station, and disposition for non-
existing/missing PPE items, for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances
of government agencies.
 City General Services Officer, being the Head of Inventory Team, to:
a. conduct a complete cycle of physical count of PPE items owned by the City
Government of Santa Rosa and render an accurate RPCPPE thereon; and
b. exclude semi/non-expendable items in the conduct of physical count and in the
preparation of the RPCPPE.
 City Accountant to draw the necessary Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to
derecognize the disposed scrap/unserviceable PPE items in the books of
accounts based on the pertinent records received from the CGSO.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
a. City Engineer, City Accountant and the City General Service Officer to conduct
inventory count of Road Network pursuant to item V.1 of COA Circular No. 2015-
008 so as to identify road components necessary for adjustments such as
impaired and unrecorded roads;
b. City Accountant and City Engineer to conduct reconciliation of records necessary
to identify possible adjustments to records, aiding both records to reflect
description of road network components that is common to all records and
reports;
c. City Engineer to prepare the complete description and cost segregation of road
components for road projects and provide the same to the City Accountant and
the City General Services Officer;
d. City General Services Officer to prepare and submit the following in prescribed
forms under COA Circular No. 2015-008:
 Report on the Local Road Network (RLRN) in the format prescribed in Annex
A of the Circular;
 Local Road Inventory and Road Map;
 Local Road Network Property Card (LRNPC) in the format prescribed in
Annex E of the Circular; and
 Report on Physical Count on Road Network System (RPCRNS) in the format
prescribed in Annex C of the Circular; and
e. City Accountant to draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record derecognized
parts of the road network system subjected to replacement and/or betterments to
come up with the correct balance of the Road Network Account and provide
complete information of the subject account in the Notes to Financial Statements
in the format prescribed in Annex B of the Circular.
Henceforth, strict compliance with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2015-008 is
hereby recommended.

We recommended that the City Mayor:
a. require the Acting City Treasurer to furnish the City Accountant of a duly certified
list showing the name of taxpayers, indicating therein the property classification,
and the amount due and collectible for the year as basis in setting up the Real
Property Tax (RPT) Receivable/Special Education Tax (SET) Receivable and its
corresponding Deferred RPT/SET Income at the beginning of the year; and
b. direct the City Accountant and Acting City Treasurer to keep and maintain
detailed records, specifically the collections and subsequent recording of the
RPT/SET income per real property classifications.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant and Acting City
Treasurer to analyze and investigate the cause/s of the deficiency of P217,911,659.33
noted between the balance of the RPT and SET Receivables and the Certified List of
Delinquent Taxes as of December 31, 2021 and make the necessary adjustments in
their records, if warranted.

We recommended that the City Mayor:
a. direct the City Accountant to immediately draw the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to
effect adjustment to accounts pertaining to the unaccomplished portion of
infrastructure projects and undelivered procured items totaling P56,412,967.66
recorded in the payable account;
b. instruct the City Accountant to refrain from recording Accounts Payable merely on
the basis of Obligation Requests and instead, ensure that all supporting
documents are attached to prove that there are actual deliveries of goods or
services or project work accomplishments; and
c. oblige the concerned officials to formulate a system of internal control whereby
procurement, receipt, delivery, distribution and consumption of expendable goods
are regularly and closely monitored.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Engineer to:
a. take appropriate action to resolve Road Right of Way (RROW) issues
immediately;
b. evaluate the viability of the infrastructure project under ID number I-2017-052 for
possible reprogramming of the fund and collection of corresponding advances
granted for the subject project; and
c. perform regular monitoring and supervision and improve its operations to
accomplish the mandated targets it committed for the year to ensure the
immediate completion of the delayed projects.
It is likewise recommended for future infrastructure projects that the City Engineer
sufficiently and adequately conduct detailed engineering investigation, survey and
design, clearances, etc. as required in the Revised Implementing Rules and
Regulation (IRR) of Republic Act (RA) No. 9184.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Information and Technology
Officer (CITO), as the procuring department, to prepare estimates judiciously by
considering factors such as, but not limited to, average monthly consumption data on
the usage of the targeted end-users/offices and conduct cost analysis on the rental
rate as compared with the current market value of acquiring the printers in
establishing the Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC).
Moreover, we recommended that the City Mayor instruct the Bids and Awards
Committee (BAC) to conduct a thorough review of the Approved Budget for Contract
(ABC) for similar undertakings so as to ensure that the most advantageous price for
the government is obtained which is in line with the provisions of Section 2 of the
Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to deny request for
additional cash advance if the Accountable/Disbursing Officer (AO/DO) has an
existing unliquidated cash advance and ensure that the cash advances granted to the
AOs/DOs do not exceed their maximum cash accountabilities. Otherwise, we
recommend the increase in the application of fidelity bonds to sufficiently cover their
cash accountabilities. Henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular
No. 97-002 and Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 on the granting of cash advance.

We recommended that the City management consider shifting to Automated Payroll
System (APS) for processing and payment of benefits of officials and employees, as
well as wages of personnel under Job Order and Contract of Service, to ensure better
control over handling of cash, administrative efficiency and mitigate the risk of loss,
misappropriation and other risks associated with manual handling of cash.

We reiterated our previous years’ recommendation that the City Mayor direct the City
Accountant and Acting City Treasurer to:
a. Trace the material variance of P118,631,979.75 between the Subsidiary Ledgers
(SL) and Cashbook balances and draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to effect the
necessary adjustment to accounts; and
b. Conduct regular reconciliation of their balances so that the adjustments made
and/or errors detected by either of the offices will be adjusted / taken up in their
respective books to ensure the correctness of the balance reported as Cash in
Bank – Local Currency, Current Account, in compliance with Section 111 of
Presidential Decree PD No. 1445.

Further, we recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant and Acting
City Treasurer to provide a written justification on the repeated non-compliance with
the previous years’ audit recommendation; otherwise, administrative sanctions shall
be imposed pursuant to Section 127 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
 Acting City Treasurer to
a. ensure that the payrolls are of duly certified and approved by respective City
Officials and Employees;
b. ensure that the amount of cash advance for the payment of salaries, wages,
allowances and honoraria are equal to the net amount of payroll for a pay
period;
c. strictly adhere to Section 5.1.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002; and
d. prepare the Report of Disbursements and forward the same to the City
Accounting Office as part of the liquidation documents of the cash advances
pursuant to Section 49 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting
System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I.
 City Accountant to
a. require accountable officers to support their liquidations of cash advance for
payment of salaries, wages and honoraria with Report of Disbursements duly
certified as to correctness by them;
b. record the liquidation reports on the Subsidiary Ledgers on the actual dates of
settlement or receipt to ensure reliable running balance of the SL;
c. observe caution on recording of transactions;
d. provide a written justification on the deviations recording of liquidation vis-à-
vis actual date of settlement; and
e. ensure strict compliance with the requirements of MNGAS for LGUs Volume I
and II on the grant, liquidation and recording of related transactions.

We recommended that the City Mayor maximize the use of appropriation/budget by
adequately conducting planning through maintenance of updated Local Development
Plan (LDIP) in order to appropriately select priority projects necessary for the
formulation of realistic Annual Investment Program (AIP) and be able to link this Plan
and Program to the budget, thus, improving its budget utilization.
It is likewise recommended that the City Development Council (CDC) evaluate and re-
assess the unimplemented Programs, Projects and Activities (PPAs) if those are still
responsive to the priority needs of the constituents of the City, otherwise reprogram
the fund.
A sound internal control policy, such as regular preparation and submission of the 20
per cent Development Fund (DF) utilization report and creation of working group to
monitor implementation of the projects, may likewise need to be developed for
monitoring purpose.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the City Mayor direct the City
Accountant, in coordination with the City Treasurer, to transfer the unutilized cash
balance of the LDRRMF to the Trust Fund books in compliance with COA Circular No.
2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and draw the corresponding Journal Entry
Voucher (JEV):
Particulars Dr. Cr.
Cash-in-Bank P85,758,675.30
Trust Liability – LDRRMF P85,758,675.30

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to ensure that the
guidelines on the recording of the receipts of funds from the National Government are
strictly adhered to. Henceforth, strictly comply with the posting and reportorial
requirement of the Department of Budget and Management (DBM) Local Budget
Circular (LBC) No. 136.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC)
of the City to:
a. consider submission of Tax Clearance Certificate by prospective suppliers in the
procurement of goods and services through alternative mode of procurement
involving significant amount consistent with Section 1 of Executive Order (EO)
398, series of 2005 so as to eliminate the risk of awarding contracts to unquaified
suppliers/contractors and to promotes procurement process efficiency;
b. include stipulation in all contracts requiring contracted suppliers/contractors to
regularly present, within the duration of the contract, (a) a tax clearance and (b) a
copy of income and business tax returns duly stamped and received by the BIR
and duly validated with the tax payments, as required under Section 3 of the same
EO; and
c. strictly observe compliance with EO 398, series of 2005.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant , together with the
concerned department heads/officials, to strictly comply with the Implementing Rules
and Regulation (IRR) of Republic Act (RA) Nos. 8291, 7875, 9679, and 11199 by
ensuring that the amount Due to Government Service Insurance System (GSIS),
Philippine Health Insurance Corporation (PhilHealth), Home Development Mutual
Fund (HDMF), Social Security System (SSS) for employees’ premium contributions
and loan repayments are remitted to respective agencies in full and within the
prescribed period.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to withhold taxes
against qualified payments transactions and strictly observe the provision of
Revenue Regulation (RR) No. 2-98, as amended by RR 11-2018.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the (a) LDRRMC, through the LDRRM Officer, to properly and effectively identify LDRRMF related PPAs to be undertaken during the budget year in order to ensure their full implementation and attainment of the expected outcomes within the targeted time frame; (b) Municipal Accountant to  prepare the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF monthly instead of semi-annual as required by COA Circular No. 2012-002; (c) LDRRM Officer to cause the submission of the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to Audit Team and DILG-LGOO; and (d) Local Finance Committee to observe prudence on the submission of required reports to oversight agencies as prescribed by the guidelines.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer to (a) require all RCO to turn over their collections intact and daily or not later than the next banking day to the Municipal Treasurer’s Office; and (b) deposit all collections intact regularly and within the prescribed period to the authorized government depository bank.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the LCE require the (a) Inventory Committee and GSO to (i) conduct physical inventory of all PPE of the Municipality on or before January 31 of each year, conclude said inventory count for CY 2021 and immediately submit the related reports to the Audit Team; (ii) reconcile the resultant inventory reports with the Accounting records; and (b) GSO to update PPE records such as Property Cards for the proper control and monitoring of the agency property and equipment.  We also recommended that the management consider the one-time cleaning of PPE account balances to improve the reliability of the PPE accounts.

We recommended that the LCE direct the (a) Chairman, BAC and Head, BAC Secretariat to (i) submit the copies of lacking documentary requirements for the above procurements; and (ii) henceforth, ensure compliance with the pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 9184 and its IRR and COA Circular No. 2012-001 in all procurements through public bidding; (b) Municipal Accountant to strengthen her review function on the requisite documentation of procurements; (c) Municipal Engineer to closely monitor/supervise project implementation to ensure completion within the targeted time for the immediate use of the intended beneficiaries; and (d) LDC through the MPDO to consider the immediate reprogramming to other priority undertakings of projects that determined to be unviable for implementation during the budget year.

We recommended that the (a) LSB (a) ensure that (i) SEF expenditures are supported with information that they are net of the DepEd’s budgetary provision for the same or related item(s) in its budget; and (ii) the guidelines on SEF operations, budgeting, and utilization are being adhered to; (b) submit the lacking documents noted in audit;  and (c) monitor the SEF properties by requesting the Inventory Committee to complete the validation exercise and fix the accountability of the property end-users by requiring them to acknowledge the PAR.

We commended the management for its unceasing efforts to comply with R.A. No. 9003 or the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000.  We further recommended that the LCE direct the MENRO to implement alternative waste disposal methods such as reuse and recycling that will further reduce residual wastes.

We recommended and the LCE agreed that the Acting Municipal Treasurer, Human Resource Management Officer III and Municipal Engineer should submit regularly the copies of Contracts and Purchase Orders together with its supporting documents within the prescribed period.

We recommend that the GFPS through the GAD Focal Person (a) continue its efforts to improve gender mainstreaming to achieve full compliance with the provisions of relevant laws and regulations; (b) inform the concerned DILG Office, in writing, of changes introduced in the DILG-endorsed GPB; and (c) ensure the complete implementation of activities indicated in the GPB to maximize the intervention in addressing gender-issues in the Municipality.

We commended the management for its continuous efforts to comply with the COVID-19 vaccination program guidelines, a way of providing protection to their constituents from adverse effects of the virus.  We also recommended that the (a) management continue to administer vaccines efficiently so that local herd immunity can be attained in the soonest time possible; and (b) Chairman, BAC and Municipal Health Officer submit the lacking requirements in the procurement of ancillary supplies as discussed above.

We recommended and the LCE agreed that the Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer should continue to comply with the BIR rules and regulations on the withholding and remittance of taxes on compensation, value added taxes, percentage taxes and expanded taxes from government money payments.

We recommended and the LCE agreed that the Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer should continue to comply with the R.A. No. 8291 or GSIS Act of 1997 in the withholding of premiums and loan amortizations from employees’ compensation together with the government’s share and its timely remittances to GSIS.

We recommended and the LCE agreed that the Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer should continue to comply with the R.A. No. 9679 or HDMF law of 2009 on the withholding of contributions and loan amortizations from employees’ compensation together with the government’s share and its timely remittances to HDMF.

We recommended and the LCE agreed that the Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer should continue to comply with the R.A. No. 7875 as amended by R.A. Nos. 9241 and 10606 or the National Health Insurance Act of 2013 on the withholding of contributions from employees’ compensation together with the government’s share and its timely remittances to PHIC.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the new Municipal Treasurer who agreed to deposit all collections intact regularly and within the prescribed period to the authorized government depository bank pursuant to Section 69 of PD 1445, Section 66 of the GAM for LGUs and Section 30 of the JC 1-81 of COA and MOF to protect government funds and to avoid possible loss, theft or misappropriation.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the 

(a) Accounting and Treasury Departments to periodically coordinate to reconcile records and determine the existence of stale checks; 

(b) Municipal Treasurer to send written notices to the concerned payees before the checks become stale; and 

(c) Municipal Accountant to take up the necessary adjustment for stale checks in its books of account.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

Direct the Property Officer 
(1) to conduct physical count of all inventory accounts and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) to the Auditor

(2) to submit promptly the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMIs) of all inventories issued to the Accounting unit to record the issuance to the inventory account and the corresponding expense accounts, and

(3) to maintain Stock Cards for every item; and
observations.managementAction:
Gradual counting only due to non-simultaneous physical count report given by different departments.

Per Municipal Accountant, only RSMIs pertaining to purchases of medicines were submitted to Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:

Instruct the head of the GSO to 
(i) update and maintain the required records, reports and issue the necessary Property Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR) for those issued PPEs to end users as required in Section 259, Chapter 7, of the GAM for LGUs, Volume I; and 
(ii) complete the maintenance and updating of PC records of the said office;
observations.managementAction: To this date, the reports are still being transferred from ARE to PAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to fully insure all its insurable properties to protect it against risk of loss/damage arising from fire or other fortuitous event pursuant to the Property Insurance Law.
observations.managementAction: Due to lack of personnel.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Social Worker to 

(a) submit the lacking documents to facilitate the determination of the regularity of the transactions; 

(b) ensure that the required valid documents for each type of transaction are submitted/attached to the DVs before processing the claims and allowing payment; and 

(c) MSWD and OSCA to continuously coordinate and update the list of eligible recipients for cash stipend.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the 

(a) Barangay Officials concerned to strictly implement the no littering mandate law within their jurisdiction and impose the corresponding penalty, if warranted; and 

(b) OIC-MENRO to fully operate the disposal/composting facilities to avoid or lessen the payment for tipping fees.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC to prepare the PMR and submit the same to the GPPB in printed and electronic format within the prescribed period.

We recommended and the Management agreed to continue complying with Section 6 of RA 8291, otherwise known as the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) Act of 1997, on proper deductions and remittances of contributions and loan amortizations to GSIS.

We recommended and the Management agreed to continue complying with the regulations on withholding taxes on salaries, benefits, and procurement of goods and services, proper deductions of contributions and loan amortization and the subsequent remittance thereof pursuant to BIR Revenue Regulations No. 02-1998, Republic Act No. 8424, Section 7 of Revenue Regulations (RR) No. 1-2013 and Section 5 of RR No. 11-2019, PhilHealth Circular No. 1, s. 2014 dated January 9, 2014 and IRR of National Health Insurance Act of 2013, and Section 23 of Republic Act No. 9679 and Section E.4 of HDMF Circular No. 275.
c) Require the reconciliation between the results of the actual physical inventory report with the accounting record pursuant to  Sections 119 and 120, Volume I, NGAS; and
observations.managementAction: Update records, reconcile with actual physical inventory.
observations.reason: (Results of inventory taking were not reported)

observations.recommendation: d) Require the Accountant to review the computation of depreciation expenses and ensure that the same were based on the existing depreciable PPEs of the municipal government.
observations.managementAction: Use existing property, plant and equipment as basis in computing depreciation
observations.reason: (Results of inventory taking were not reported)

observations.recommendation:
1.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the new
Municipal Treasurer who agreed to deposit all collections intact regularly
and within the prescribed period to the authorized government
depository bank pursuant to Section 69 of PD 1445, Section 66 of the
GAM for LGUs and Section 30 of the JC 1-81 of COA and MOF to
protect government funds and to avoid possible loss, theft or
misappropriation.

2.7 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the (a)
Accounting and Treasury Departments to periodically coordinate to
reconcile records and determine the existence of stale checks; (b)
Municipal Treasurer to send written notices to the concerned payees
before the checks become stale; and (c) Municipal Accountant to take up
the necessary adjustment for stale checks in its books of account.

3.5 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive :
a. Direct the Property Officer (1) to conduct physical count of all
inventory accounts and submit the Report on the Physical Count of
Inventories (RPCI) to the Auditor, (2) to submit promptly the
Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMIs) of all inventories
issued to the Accounting unit to record the issuance to the inventory
account and the corresponding expense accounts fairly present the
financial statements, and (3) to maintain Stock Cards for every
item; and
b. Instruct the Property Officer to coordinate with the Accountant and
other officials concerned to ensure that the appropriate internal
control system are installed to monitor, control and ensure
recording of issued inventories and the corresponding expense
account pursuant to the procedures provided in Sections 189, 194,
and 196, Chapter 6, GAM for LGUs, Volume 1, cited above.

4.14 We reiterated our recommendations and the Local Chief Executive
agreed to:
a) Instruct the head of the GSO to (i) update and maintain the required
records, reports and issue the necessary Property Acknowledgement
Receipt (PAR) for those issued PPEs to end users as required in
Section 259, Chapter 7, of the GAM for LGUs, Volume I; and (ii)
complete the maintenance and updating of PC records of the said
office;
b) Direct the GSO/Inventory Committee to complete the physical count
of PPE and prepare/submit the corresponding Report on Physical
Count of PPE required under Section 266 of the said GAM for
LGUs;
c) Require the reconciliation between the results of the actual physical
count report with the accounting record pursuant to Section 269 of
the said GAM for LGUs; and
d) Require the Accountant to review the computation of depreciation
expenses and ensure that the same were based on the existing
depreciable PPEs of the municipal government.

5.5 We recommended and the Management agreed to fully insure all its
insurable properties to protect it against risk of loss/damage arising from
fire or other fortuitous event pursuant to the Property Insurance Law.

6.7 In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Direct the Property officer to (a) investigate and determine the
responsible official for the loss and coordinate with concerned
official for the proper documentation, immediate
replacement/settlement of missing equipment and to file charges, if
warranted; and (b) file the notice of loss and request for relief from
property accountability; and
b. Direct all property accountable officers under his jurisdiction to
safeguard government property and strictly follow the regulations
on property management cited above.

7.10 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Social Worker to (a)
submit the lacking documents to facilitate the determination of the regularity
of the transactions; (b) ensure that the required valid documents for each
type of transaction are submitted/attached to the DVs before processing the
claims and allowing payment; and (c) MSWD and OSCA to continuously
coordinate and update the list of eligible recipients for cash stipend.

9.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the (a) Barangay
Officials concerned to strictly implement the no littering mandate law
within their jurisdiction and impose the corresponding penalty, if
warranted; and (b) OIC-MENRO to fully operate the
disposal/composting facilities to avoid or lessen the payment for tipping
fees.

10.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC to prepare
the PMR and submit the same to the GPPB in printed and electronic
format within the prescribed period.

12.13We recommended and the Management agreed to continue complying
with the regulations on withholding taxes on salaries, benefits, and
procurement of goods and services, proper deductions of contributions
and loan amortization and the subsequent remittance thereof pursuant to
BIR Revenue Regulations No. 02-1998, Republic Act No. 8424, Section 7
of Revenue Regulations (RR) No. 1-2013 and Section 5 of RR No. 11-
2019, PhilHealth Circular No. 1, s. 2014 dated January 9, 2014 and IRR
of National Health Insurance Act of 2013, and Section 23 of Republic Act
No. 9679 and Section E.4 of HDMF Circular No. 275.

We recommended that the City Mayor undertake the following courses of action:

a. Direct the City Accountant to immediately submit to the Audit Team all of the
necessary documents such as Delivery Receipts, Acknowledgement Receipts,
Contractor’s Statement of Work Accomplished, Certificate of Inspection and
Acceptance and other equivalent documents to support the recording of
Accounts Payable. Also, submit documents to prove the procurement,
distribution and consumption of the consumable supplies amounting to
P123,575,215.24;

b. Direct the City Accountant to conduct an analysis of Accounts Payable and
effect adjustments thereon for identified undelivered goods, unaccomplished
services and/or unaccomplished civil works;

c. Revert to the General or Special Education Funds of the City all unsupported
Accounts Payable;

d. Require the City Accountant to conduct further analysis on the recorded
Accounts Payable amounting to P8,076,075.00, which remain in the books for
two years or more. If found that the claims are legitimate and supported with
complete documents and valid claims, immediately settle the obligations.
Otherwise, revert the long outstanding payables and prepare the necessary
Journal Entry Voucher (JEV);

e. Instruct the City Accountant to refrain from recording Accounts Payable
merely on the basis of Obligation Requests, and instead, ensure that all
supporting documents are attached to prove that there are actual deliveries of
goods or services or project work accomplishments; and

f. Oblige the concerned officials to formulate a system of internal control
whereby procurement, receipt, delivery, distribution and consumption of
expendable goods are regularly and closely monitored.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:

a. City Engineer to prepare the complete description and cost segregation of
road components for road projects and provide the same to the City
Accountant and the CGSO;

b. CGSO to prepare and submit the:








B


RLRN in the format prescribed in Annex A of COA Circular No. 2015-008






Local Road Inventory and Road Map



LRNPC in the format prescribed in Annex E of the said Circular and


RPCRNS in the format prescribed in Annex C of the Circular; and

c. City Accountant to disclose the total road network system in the Notes to
Financial Statements in the format prescribed in Annex B of COA Circular No.
2015-008. Likewise, make the necessary adjustment/s in the books of
accounts, the derecognition of the cost of replaced items/components to come
up with the correct balance of the Road Networks account.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the Acting City Treasurer to furnish
the City Accountant a duly certified list showing the names of taxpayers, and the
amount due and collectible for the year as basis in setting up the RPT and SET
Receivables and its corresponding Deferred RPT and SET Income at the beginning
of the year. Thereafter, we recommended that the City Accountant prepare JEV
setting up the RPT/SET Receivables and Deferred Income at the beginning of the
year based on the records submitted by the Acting City Treasurer, and strictly
comply with Section 20 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I in setting up and in the
subsequent recording of the RPT/SET Receivable accounts and its corresponding
Deferred Income accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management set specific policy guidelines in the grant of financial assistance.
